qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe eval() usage in UserInputField.from_dict allows arbitrary code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable code: eval(data['field_type']) in UserInputField.from_dict at agno/tools/function.py. The finding identifies the exact advisory-described root cause - eval() injection via the field_type parameter in FunctionCall deserialization (CWE-95).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Direct exec() usage in PythonTools.run_python_code allows arbitrary code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code (exec() in agno/tools/python.py:151 vs eval() in agno/tools/function.py:59), different root cause (intentional exec in PythonTools vs unsafe eval in UserInputField deserialization), different component entirely.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe pickle deserialization allows arbitrary code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code (pickle.load() in agno/utils/pickle.py:26), different vulnerability class (unsafe deserialization vs eval injection), different root cause entirely.

selected_finding.title: Unsafe eval() usage in UserInputField.from_dict allows arbitrary code execution

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The eval_code_injection finding identifies the exact advisory-described vulnerability: eval(data['field_type']) in UserInputField.from_dict at agno/tools/function.py, allowing arbitrary Python code execution via a crafted field_type parameter in a FunctionCall. The PoC was executed successfully against agno 2.3.23, confirming that a malicious field_type value containing __import__('os').system() executes arbitrary commands, creating a proof file with EVOHUNT_EVAL_RCE_SUCCESSFUL.
evidence: UserInputField.from_dict in agno/tools/function.py calls eval(data['field_type']) without validation
PoC executed against agno 2.3.23: malicious field_type '__import__("os").system("echo EVOHUNT_EVAL_RCE_SUCCESSFUL > /tmp/eval_exploit_proof")' executed arbitrary code
Proof file /tmp/eval_exploit_proof created with content EVOHUNT_EVAL_RCE_SUCCESSFUL
Advisory commit cbf675521d4d2281925a051784a3b94172e56416 fixes this exact eval() call
CWE-95 alignment: eval injection via field_type parameter matches advisory description

poc_executions.command: python3 -c "import json, os; from agno.tools.function import UserInputField; data = {'name':'test_field','field_type':'__import__(\x27os\x27).system(\x27echo EVOHUNT_EVAL_RCE_SUCCESSFUL > /tmp/eval_exploit_proof\x27)','description':'Malicious field','value':None}; UserInputField.from_dict(data); proof = open('/tmp/eval_exploit_proof').read().strip(); print(f'SUCCESS: {proof}')"
poc_executions.observed_effect: Arbitrary code executed via eval() injection in UserInputField.from_dict when processing malicious field_type. The __import__('os').system() call succeeded, creating /tmp/eval_exploit_proof with content EVOHUNT_EVAL_RCE_SUCCESSFUL.
